ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'salad food product' class (FOODON_00002539). No specific code exists for a customisable egg-topped mixed-vegetable salad without dressing; closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers the 'Salads' category. No narrower code exists for a customisable egg-topped salad without sauce; closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept identified for this customisable salad preparation; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: four variable toppings (e.g. cherry tomatoes, cucumber, carrot, corn, croutons, cheese, nuts)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of mixed-vegetable salad with egg and four toppings, approximately 250 g, served without sauce or dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Salad with egg (4 toppings), no sauce — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Salad with egg (4 toppings), no sauce' (cr_id F08010048). All per-100 g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable salad formulation. GI estimated at 38 based on the closest analogous entry in the University of Sydney GI Database: mixed green salad with vegetables (GI ~35–40, low category). The dominant carbohydrate contributors are non-starchy vegetables and egg, both of which have negligible glycaemic impact. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 7.78 g) / 100 ≈ 2.9 (serving = 250 g, carbohydrate = 3.11 g/100 g × 250 g = 7.78 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (135 mg/100 g) with no added sauce; estimated potassium ~225 mg/100 g yields a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles.
hypertension.guidance: This salad is suitable for individuals managing hypertension; however, patients should avoid high-sodium toppings such as processed meats, pickled vegetables, or cured cheeses, as these can substantially increase the sodium load of the dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (3.11 g/100 g), low sugar (2.53 g/100 g), low estimated GI (~38), and dietary fibre (1.67 g/100 g) collectively support postprandial glucose control.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This salad is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low glycaemic load (~2.9 per 250 g serving); patients should limit starchy toppings such as croutons or sweetcorn to preserve the low glycaemic profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (2.3 g/100 g), low saturated fat (~0.6 g/100 g), no added sauce, and a vegetable-dominant base with dietary fibre (1.67 g/100 g) and iron (0.83 mg/100 g) support cardiovascular health.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This salad is suitable for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; patients should select heart-healthy toppings such as nuts or avocado and avoid processed meat toppings that increase saturated fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Vegetable base provides antioxidants and fibre; however, no confirmed anti-inflammatory factors (omega-3, polyphenols, cruciferous vegetables, or anti-inflammatory spices) are present in the base formulation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This salad is not contraindicated for rheumatoid arthritis but does not provide sufficient anti-inflammatory benefit in its base form; patients should add omega-3-rich toppings (e.g. walnuts, salmon) and monitor nightshade toppings (tomato, bell pepper) for individual symptom triggers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salad with Egg (4 Toppings), No Sauce is a customisable mixed-vegetable salad served without any dressing or sauce, featuring a hard-boiled or sliced egg and a choice of four variable toppings. The base typically comprises mixed salad leaves, cucumber, tomato, and other fresh non-starchy vegetables, assembled raw and served chilled or at room temperature. The texture is crisp and fresh, with a mild, neutral flavour profile that varies depending on the toppings selected. Per 100 g, the dish provides 47 kcal, 3.51 g protein, 2.3 g fat, 3.11 g carbohydrate, 2.53 g sugar, 1.67 g dietary fibre, and 135 mg sodium, as recorded in the Singapore HPB Foodo database. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 38 (low), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 2.9 per standard 250 g serving. Clinically, this salad is well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its very low carbohydrate content, low sodium, low fat, and absence of added sauce. The egg component contributes protein and micronutrients including vitamin D. The dish is classified as vegetarian, halal-compatible, and gluten-free in its base form. Topping selection significantly affects the nutritional and clinical profile; starchy, processed, or high-sodium toppings should be avoided for health-sensitive individuals.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id F08010048) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists for this specific formulation; this is clearly documented.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans), cholesterol, fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E), B-vitamin panel (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate), and moisture data for this customisable salad formulation are not individually reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (F08010048) as of 2026, and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific mixed-dish formulation. Limited published data exists for this specific customisable preparation; the -1 sentinel is used to indicate data not available rather than a confirmed zero. 2. saturated_fat_g (0.6 g/100 g) is estimated from the egg and vegetable fat composition using USDA FoodData Central reference values for hard-boiled egg and mixed salad vegetables; this is an approximation. 3. potassium_mg (225 mg/100 g) and vitamin_c_mg (11 mg/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference values for mixed salad vegetables and egg, consistent with the HPB health suitability notes in the input record. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (~38, low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed green salad with vegetables; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific formulation. 5. This dish is classified as Western/International cuisine; Singaporean multilingual naming requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply. 6. Dietary flag is_gluten_free and is_dairy_free are set for the base formulation only; toppings may introduce gluten (croutons) or dairy (cheese). 7. No core macro (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) is zero; zero-macro justification rule is not triggered.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Added justification for all -1 values in the 'notes' field to address g3_numeric_hygiene failures from iteration 1.
Iteration 2: Changed ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broad' to 'closest_parent' to use only allowed values per g5_ontology_honesty validator rules.

Iteration 3: Expanded -1 sentinel documentation in 'notes' to explicitly name every field set to -1 and state the reason (not reported in HPB Foodo F08010048 and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific mixed-dish formulation) and confirm the source consulted, fully satisfying g3_numeric_hygiene requirements. Confirmed ontology match types remain 'closest_parent' (allowed value) for both FoodOn and FoodEx2.
